heading: Thorpdale South vs Thowgla Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thorpdale South, Victoria and Thowgla Valley, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Thorpdale South has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Thowgla Valley (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thorpdale South or Thowgla Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thorpdale South has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Thowgla Valley scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thorpdale South scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Thowgla Valley scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thorpdale South or Thowgla Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Thorpdale South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
